concept.title: 01 The FLXfinish+ function

abstract.p: FLXfinish+ is a new print option for the Colorado system: it is a mixed matte-gloss print mode.
Example of a print in FLXfinish+ mode:
An additional print mode is available: FLXfinish+. It contains 22 passes: 8 matte, 6 gloss and 8 in the twilight area. The print speed is 7 m2/hr (75 ft2/hr).
The new print mode is supported in the latest version of the job submitters. It also requires new media profiles.

section.title: Use of the printheads
section.p: In this print mode the printhead is split into 3 parts: print the gloss part of the image, a non used part and the part to print matte part of the image.

li.p: Gloss part of the printhead. No Pin curing is required.
The twilight part of the printhead. It is not used to print in this print mode.
Matte part of the printhead. Pin curing is required in this area.

section.title: Additional mirror
section.p: To support the Pin curing of the matte part of the image an addition mirror is required. It is mounted at the left hand side of the printhead carriage.
The light, that is guided via the mirror is partly blocked as you can in the photo below.

section.title: FLXfinish+ strip
section.p: An additional FLXfinish+ strip is printed at the right hand side of the image, between the image and the AAC markers.
Aim of the strip: keep the nozzles of the printheads in condition.
Especially the nozzles that are not used:

li.p: and the "not used" nozzles in the middle of the printhead.

section.p: The strip requires a width of 15mm (, so this reduces the maximum printable width, in FLXfinish+ mode, with 15mm.
